mission.title: Install and Configure Easegress on Kubernetes
mission.description: Easegress is a Cloud Native traffic orchestration system that provides high availability, traffic management, and observability features. Installing Easegress allows you to efficiently manage and route traffic in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Easegress Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Easegress Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add megaease https://charts.kubesphere.io/stable
```

steps.title: Install Easegress using Helm
steps.description:
Install Easegress in a new namespace called 'easegress' with the following command:
```bash
helm install easegress megaease/easegress --namespace easegress --create-namespace --version v2.10.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Easegress pods are running successfully by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace easegress
```

steps.title: Expose the Easegress service
steps.description:
To access the Easegress service, you can port-forward it to your local machine:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/easegress --namespace easegress 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Easegress will show the pods running in the 'easegress' namespace when you execute 'kubectl get pods'. You should also be able to access the service via port-forwarding on localhost:8080.
codeSnippets: helm install easegress megaease/easegress --namespace easegress --create-namespace --version v2.10.1

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
To cleanly remove the Easegress deployment, execute the following command to uninstall the Helm release:
```bash
helm uninstall easegress --namespace easegress
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Easegress:
```bash
kubectl delete crd easegresses.megaease.com
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace easegress
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the Easegress namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace easegress
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of the Easegress deployment:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace easegress -o yaml > easegress-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Easegress release to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade easegress megaease/easegress --namespace easegress --version v2.11.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Easegress pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace easegress
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Easegress pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace easegress
```
Look for any configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Easegress service, ensure that the service is running and check the port-forwarding command:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace easegress
kubectl port-forward svc/easegress --namespace easegress 8080:80
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are failing due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment easegress --namespace easegress
```
Adjust the resource limits in the deployment if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade command fails, check for any existing Helm release issues:
```bash
helm list --namespace easegress
```
If necessary, rollback to the previous version:
```bash
helm rollback easegress <revision-number> --namespace easegress
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy Easegress.
